chosen.headline: Sensex jumps 444 points and Nifty reclaims 24,000 as the market snaps a two-day slide

story.headline: Sensex jumps 444 points and Nifty reclaims 24,000 as the market snaps a two-day slide
story.what_happened: Indian benchmark indices ended a two-day losing streak on Wednesday. The Sensex rose 443.97 points (+0.58%) to close at 76,922.64 and the Nifty 50 added 140.85 points (+0.59%) to end at 24,005.85, moving back above the psychologically watched 24,000 level. The rebound was broad, powered by FMCG, auto and realty names, and helped the benchmarks recover the ground lost in the previous two sessions.
story.why_it_matters: Reclaiming 24,000 on the Nifty after a two-session decline shows buyers stepped back in at lower levels, with strength spread across domestic-facing sectors rather than a single heavyweight. It marks a shift in short-term tone back toward the upside, even as export-led IT lagged.
story.investor_takeaway: For educational context only: a broad-based bounce that reclaims a round-number level like 24,000, led by domestic sectors while IT lags, illustrates rotation and short-term sentiment - not a directional call. Not investment advice.

Business Standard - Stock Market Close: Sensex gains 444 pts, Nifty ends at 24,006
